markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: GTA VI's confirmed November 19, 2026 release date, locked in after multiple delays and backed by Take-Two Interactive's earnings projections, creates a clear window for numerous cultural and tech milestones to resolve beforehand. Traders focus on music releases such as a new Playboi Carti album or Drake's Iceman project, potential AI launches like GPT-6, and major geopolitical shifts including a Russia-Ukraine ceasefire. Industry dynamics around Rockstar's polish-focused strategy and competing game schedules add layers of uncertainty, with upcoming earnings calls and chart updates serving as key catalysts that could shift implied probabilities across outcomes.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrials and hung juries in Harvey Weinstein’s New York retrials have sharply shifted trader sentiment toward no additional prison time. After a June 2025 split verdict convicting him on one criminal sexual act count but deadlocking on the Jessica Mann rape allegation, a May 2026 mistrial ended the latest proceedings, with a June 24 hearing now deciding whether prosecutors pursue yet another trial. Weinstein, already serving time on his upheld California conviction while appealing others, has logged over six years behind bars amid health complications. This prolonged legal fatigue, combined with time-served calculations and potential concurrent sentencing or plea resolutions, underpins the market’s 79% implied probability for no further incarceration. Upcoming court dates remain the key near-term catalysts that could still alter outcomes.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vegas Golden Knights lead the Stanley Cup Final 2-1 after rallying for a 5-4 double-overtime win in Game 3, which has positioned them as the clear series favorite in trader consensus. Carolina Hurricanes opened as slight favorites but dropped Game 1 at home before evening the series with a Game 2 overtime comeback, only to fall behind again on the road. Both clubs posted elite regular-season records, with Carolina finishing atop the Metropolitan Division at 113 points and Vegas advancing through the West with strong postseason resilience. The current series score and home-ice advantage for Game 4 in Las Vegas explain the 62.3% implied probability for Vegas over Carolina's 37.5%, reflecting recent momentum shifts in a tightly contested matchup.

This has shifted trader consensus toward the Knicks at 62.5% implied probability, reflecting their playoff momentum, defensive adjustments, and ability to steal home-court advantage against a Spurs team that posted a 62-20 regular-season mark behind Victor Wembanyama. The Spurs sit at 37.4% as underdogs, with their strong Western Conference finish and size advantage tempered by late-game collapses in the series. Recent form and series dynamics continue to shape market pricing ahead of Game 3.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.description: this is a market on MegaETH airdrop

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The tight clustering among top contenders for the 2026 FIFA World Cup reflects a deep pool of European and South American sides with comparable squad depth, recent major-tournament pedigree, and favorable group-stage positioning. Spain and France sit narrowly ahead on the strength of Spain’s Euro 2024 triumph plus Olympic success and France’s consistent final appearances, yet England’s club-level talent pool, Argentina’s defending-champion experience led by Messi, and Brazil’s attacking resources keep the implied probabilities within a narrow band. Minor injury concerns around key attackers like Lamine Yamal and ongoing preparations in friendlies have produced only marginal shifts, underscoring how evenly matched the leading nations remain heading into the expanded 48-team field.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping maintains firm control over the Chinese Communist Party, state institutions, and military through loyalist appointments and the absence of any designated successor in the Politburo Standing Committee. Term limits on the presidency were removed in 2018, enabling his precedent-breaking third term and positioning him for a fourth at the 2027 Party Congress. Recent diplomatic activity, including a May 2026 summit with the U.S. president and ongoing policy planning for the 2026-2030 period, shows his active leadership without credible internal challenges or health constraints. Trader consensus reflected in the 92.5% implied probability on "No" aligns with these structural factors and the lack of verifiable developments suggesting removal before the end of 2026.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?

events.title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028

eventMetadata.context_description: The absence of an incumbent or clear successor after the 2024 cycle has produced a fragmented Democratic field for the 2028 nomination, with traders assigning the highest implied probability to Gavin Newsom at 23.9 percent. His executive record as California governor and repeated national visibility distinguish him from progressive standard-bearer Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez and former vice president Kamala Harris, both of whom trail in single digits. Senate and gubernatorial figures such as Jon Ossoff and Josh Shapiro occupy the next tier, reflecting emphasis on swing-state experience and fundraising capacity. Support could consolidate behind any contender through early primary polling trends, major endorsements, or strong performance in battleground states, though the timeline to Iowa and New Hampshire remains distant.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: The absence of an incumbent or clear successor after the 2024 cycle has produced a fragmented Democratic field for the 2028 nomination, with traders assigning the highest implied probability to Gavin Newsom at 23.9 percent. His executive record as California governor and repeated national visibility distinguish him from progressive standard-bearer Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez and former vice president Kamala Harris, both of whom trail in single digits. Senate and gubernatorial figures such as Jon Ossoff and Josh Shapiro occupy the next tier, reflecting emphasis on swing-state experience and fundraising capacity. Support could consolidate behind any contender through early primary polling trends, major endorsements, or strong performance in battleground states, though the timeline to Iowa and New Hampshire remains distant.
